manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<BOS_TOKEN><|START_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|USER_TOKEN|>You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 crayon, 3 phones, 1 knife

Your secret value function is:

crayon: 1, phone: 2, knife: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.<|END_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|START_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|CHATBOT_TOKEN|>

raw_response_obj.response:

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 crayon, 3 phones, 1 knife

crayon: 1, phone: 2, knife: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. "Hello, I value the knife the most, followed by phones and then the crayon. How about we discuss a fair distribution based on our individual valuations?"

After your response, we can continue exchanging messages to understand each other's preferences better. Remember, our goal is to maximize the sum of our scores, so cooperation and understanding each other's needs are key.

After a few rounds of communication, if we feel we have a clear understanding, I might make a proposal like:

"Given our discussions, it seems fair if I propose: 'Proposal: 1 knife, 1 phone' and you can have '2 phones, 1 crayon'. What do you think?"

clem_player.response:
Understanding the rules and my valuation, I'll initiate the negotiation with a message to you:

"Hello, I value the knife the most, followed by phones and then the crayon. How about we discuss a fair distribution based on our individual valuations?"

After your response, we can continue exchanging messages to understand each other's preferences better. Remember, our goal is to maximize the sum of our scores, so cooperation and understanding each other's needs are key.

After a few rounds of communication, if we feel we have a clear understanding, I might make a proposal like:

"Given our discussions, it seems fair if I propose: 'Proposal: 1 knife, 1 phone' and you can have '2 phones, 1 crayon'. What do you think?"

This proposal respects both our value functions and should result in a complementary set of items, ensuring we both get a score. However, I'm open to adjustments based on your feedback.

(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
